info.title: Green Ecolution API
info.description: REST API for the Green Ecolution smart irrigation and green-space management platform. Combines IoT sensor data, route optimization, and automated maintenance scheduling to help municipalities manage urban greenery efficiently. The system uses LoRaWAN sensors to monitor soil conditions, calculates optimal watering routes, and provides fleet management capabilities.

get.description: Lightweight liveness probe for container orchestrators. Returns 200 OK while the HTTP server is responsive; performs no downstream service checks. Use /api/ready for a readiness probe that verifies critical dependencies, or /api/v1/info/services for a deep services health check.

Readiness probe for container orchestrators. Verifies that critical dependencies (the database) are reachable and returns 200 only when the service can serve traffic; returns 503 otherwise so the pod is pulled from the load balancer. Optional services (auth, MQTT) are reported by /api/v1/info/services and do not gate readiness.

200.description: Service is ready to serve traffic

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all tree clusters with a compact representation including region info. Optional filter parameters (watering_status, region) narrow the result; array parameters are repeatable.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Free-text search across cluster name and address.

post.description: Creates a new tree cluster with the given properties and returns the created resource.

get.description: Returns a convex-hull boundary polygon (GeoJSON, buffered by a fixed margin in meters) around the trees of each non-archived cluster. Not paginated.
Returns lightweight markers (id, name, lat, lng, status, tree_count) for all non-archived clusters with a centroid. Not paginated.
Counts of non-archived clusters per watering status, plus total clusters and total trees in clusters.
Returns a single tree cluster by its unique identifier, including fully resolved tree objects.

put.description: Updates an existing tree cluster identified by its ID and returns the updated resource.

delete.description: Deletes a tree cluster identified by its ID.

get.description: Returns aggregated statistics including resource counts, water consumption, and per-region/vehicle breakdowns.
Returns version, git, and map configuration metadata.
Returns the map center coordinates and bounding box.
Returns server OS, hostname, and network information.
Returns health status of all connected services (database, routing, etc.).
Returns counts of all managed resources.
Returns all registered plugins.

post.description: Register a new external plugin and receive authentication tokens.

get.description: Returns plugin information by slug.

post.description: Send a keepalive heartbeat for a registered plugin.
Refresh authentication token for a plugin.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all geographic regions used to organize tree clusters.
Returns a single region by its unique identifier.
Returns a paginated list of all LoRaWAN sensors.

post.description: Creates a sensor record in `Prepared` state. The sensor must later be bound to a tree via `POST /sensors/{sensor_id}/activate` before it starts receiving data.

get.description: Returns every sensor model registered in the catalogue along with its abilities (e.g. soil tension at 30/60/90 cm).
Returns a single sensor by its EUI identifier.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a sensor by its EUI identifier.

post.summary: Activate a prepared sensor by binding it to a tree
post.description: Transitions a sensor from `Prepared` to `Offline` and attaches it to the given tree. Idempotent if the sensor is already attached to the same tree.

409.description: Conflict: sensor or tree already linked

get.description: Returns all historical data readings for a sensor.

get.summary: Get the tree associated with a sensor
get.description: Retrieves the tree linked to the given sensor. Returns 404 if the sensor or its associated tree does not exist.

404.description: Sensor or associated tree not found

put.summary: Move an activated sensor to a different tree
put.description: Re-links an already activated sensor to `tree_id`. Rejects a tree that already has a different sensor and a sensor that is not yet activated. Idempotent if the sensor is already linked to that tree.

409.description: Conflict: sensor not activated or tree already linked

delete.summary: Remove a sensor's tree link and reset it to prepared
delete.description: Detaches the sensor from its tree and deactivates it, returning it to the `Prepared` state. Idempotent for an already prepared sensor.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all trees with their associated sensor data. Optional `q` parameter case-insensitively filters by tree number or species. Optional filter parameters (watering_status, has_cluster, planting_year) narrow the result; array parameters are repeatable.

post.description: Creates a new tree with location and optional cluster or sensor association.

get.summary: List tree markers in a bounding box
get.description: Returns minimal tree markers (id, lat, lng, watering_status, number, has_sensor) intersecting the given bounding box. Optional filter parameters narrow the result. Not paginated — the bounding box bounds the result.
Finds trees closest to a given coordinate.
Returns a list of distinct planting years across all trees.
Returns a single tree by its ID, including associated sensor data.

put.description: Performs a full replacement update of a tree by its ID.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a tree by its ID.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of registered users.
Returns a filtered list of users with a specific role.
Returns a paginated list of active vehicles.
Returns a paginated list of decommissioned vehicles.

post.description: Moves a vehicle to the archived state.

get.description: Looks up a vehicle by its license plate number.
Returns a single vehicle by its ID.

put.description: Updates the details of an existing vehicle.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of watering plans with embedded vehicles and clusters.

post.description: Creates a new watering plan with clusters, vehicles, and user assignments.

get.description: Downloads the optimized watering route as a GPX file.

post.description: Calculates and previews an optimized watering route without creating a plan.

get.description: Returns full watering plan detail including evaluation values.

put.description: Updates the details or status of an existing watering plan.

Request body for `POST /sensors/{sensor_id}/activate` - binds a prepared

items.description: Represents a watering vehicle used for urban green-space irrigation.

LorawanCredentialsRequest.description:
LoRaWAN credentials supplied when registering a new sensor. `app_key` is
write-only and never echoed back in responses.

LorawanInfoResponse.description:
LoRaWAN connection details exposed publicly. The `config` map is filtered
via [`redact_lorawan_config`] to strip OTAA / session keys and the device
password before serialization.

tags.name: Regions
tags.description: Manage geographic regions used to group tree clusters. Regions define administrative boundaries for organizing green spaces.

tags.name: Tree Clusters
tags.description: Manage tree clusters — logical groupings of trees that share soil conditions and watering schedules. Clusters are the primary unit for watering plan assignments.

tags.name: Trees
tags.description: Manage individual trees including their species, location, planting year, and associated sensors. Trees can be assigned to clusters for grouped watering management.

tags.name: Vehicles
tags.description: Manage watering vehicles (transporters and trailers) including their water capacity, dimensions, and availability status. Vehicles can be archived when decommissioned.

tags.name: Sensors
tags.description: Access LoRaWAN sensor data for soil moisture monitoring. Sensors are linked to individual trees and provide real-time environmental readings.

tags.name: Watering Plans
tags.description: Create and manage watering plans that combine tree clusters, vehicles, and optimized routes. Plans track status from planning through execution.

tags.name: Evaluation
tags.description: Aggregated statistics and evaluation data across all managed resources. Provides insights on watering plan coverage by region and vehicle usage.

tags.name: Info
tags.description: Application metadata including version information, server status, map configuration, service health, and data statistics.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User registration and role management. Authentication is handled directly against Keycloak.

tags.name: Plugins
tags.description: Plugin registration and lifecycle management. External plugins can register, authenticate, and maintain heartbeat connections.
